name: RABBIT-BUG-5
title: rabbit-bug skill not triggered on fresh session when user says 'file a bug'
description: On a clean session start, when the user explicitly says 'file a bug', the rabbit-bug skill is not detected and invoked before action. Instead Claude proceeds directly to implementation (e.g. exploring registry.json) without calling the Skill tool first. The using-superpowers skill mandates: invoke relevant skills BEFORE any response or action, even at 1% chance of applicability. 'File a bug' is an unambiguous trigger phrase for rabbit-bug. Observed session: user said 'file a bug on those two errors', Claude did NOT invoke rabbit-bug skill, explored registry instead, and only invoked the skill after being called out. Root cause unknown — could be skill description mismatch, skill trigger logic gap, or Claude reasoning error. Must investigate and fix so the skill fires automatically on any bug-filing intent without user prompting.
